rawtext.text:
Don't stand in the open like that — you make a lovely target.

Kestrel Thorn. I watch the treeline so the rest of them can sleep. Been counting campfires in the hills for a fortnight, and every night there's more. The Corrupted aren't wandering. They're §omustering§r.

Three nights ago I got close enough to one of their camps to hear them. They don't talk. They §ohum§r — all of them, the same low note, like a swarm deciding something.

And here's what froze me: one of them was wearing my old captain's coat. Lost him five years back in the eastern pass, buried him myself. Whatever is filling those bodies, it isn't picky about where it finds them.

The big one — the one with the banner — the others part around him like water. If we cut §ohim§r down, I'd wager the rest lose their nerve. If they still have nerve to lose.

Two things, and both matter.

§6Perimeter Patrol§r — walk the four watch posts at the compass points of the village. I need fresh eyes confirming my markers still stand. Finish the circuit and my §aScout's Beacon§r is yours — it has pulled me out of the dark more times than I can count.

§6The Last Caravan§r — Orin's supply cart is the final one before the siege. The Corrupted will hit it; they always do. Accept the job, then tell me to signal the caravan when your blade is loose in its sheath. §oKeep Orin alive.§r
